Article Title: Petitioner hurls papers, 'orders' SC to file FIR
Article Text:
Supreme Court

Despite criminal contempt, SC refrains from taking action

NEW DELHI: A "petitioner-in-person", Prabal Pratap, on Friday caught a Supreme Court bench and advocates by surprise when he "ordered" the judges to register a case against a police officer in Lucknow, threw a bunch of papers in the courtroom and then went on to swear at the CJI.Appearing along with co-petitioner Chandra Bhan before a bench of Justices K V Viswanathan and Alok Aradeh, Pratap said: "Mr Judicial Servant, I order you to order registration of FIR against ACP, Vikas Nagar, Lucknow.""You are ordering us?" asked a surprised Justice Viswanathan. Unfazed, Pratap who, like Chandra Bhan, was attired like an advocate, continued brandishing 185 pages of documents against Duplex Technologies, which, he alleged, is running a syndicate.He tossed the bundle into the air. As the papers scattered, he then used a cuss word, prompting security personnel to physically remove him from the courtroom.However, despite the criminal contempt, the bench did not initiate any action. The bench said, "As far as the merit of this case is concerned, we have perused the records, we find no good ground to interfere with the impugned judgement/order(s). The special leave petition is, accordingly, dismissed," it said.Pratap had challenged Allahabad high court's April 6 order refusing to entertain his writ petition seeking quashing of the February 21 order of special chief judicial magistrate (custom), Lucknow, which had converted his application under Sec 173(3) of Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita into a private complaint case along with subsequent proceedings arising from the case Prabal Pratap Vs Duplex Technologies Services.He had also sought a direction to the Lucknow police commissioner to take over the investigations into his allegations against Duplex Technologies Services. The high court had dismissed his writ petition saying the chief judicial magistrate's order could be challenged in accordance with law.
Article Summary: "Petitioner-in-person" Prabal Pratap caught a Supreme Court bench and advocates by surprise. He threw a bunch of papers in the courtroom and then went on to swear at the CJI. Despite the criminal contempt, the bench did not initiate any action.

Article Title: TV actor Rohit Chandel arrested in Mumbai for sexually harassing 16-year-old
Article Text:
The actor, Rohit Chandel (29), played Baji Rao in Kashibai Bajirao Ballal and Dhawal Makwana in Pandya Store.





MUMBAI: A television actor has been arrested by the city police for allegedly stalking, repeatedly calling, harassing and assaulting a 16-year-old girl in an eastern suburb. Police have booked him under the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (Pocso) Act.The actor, Rohit Chandel (29), played Baji Rao in Kashibai Bajirao Ballal and Dhawal Makwana in Pandya Store.Besides the stringent Pocso Act , Pant Nagar Police have also invoked the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita(BNS) sections 78 and 115 (2) for stalking and causing hurt.Chandel was arrested from his Dahisar home on Friday and produced before the special Pocso court which remanded him to police custody.According to the complaint lodged by a resident of an eastern suburb, the accused had allegedly repeatedly called the minor on her mobile phone from his number as well as several other numbers, allegedly harassing her.The complaint further alleges that on July 5, he confronted the girl near her residential building, chased her, picked up an argument, abused her, and assaulted her by hitting her with his hands.Police said the minor and the accused were acquainted. “Following the complaint, a case was registered and the accused has been placed under arrest. Further probe is underway in the case,’’ said an official.
Article Summary: The actor, Rohit Chandel (29), played Baji Rao in Kashibai Bajirao Ballal and Dhawal Makwana in Pandya Store. Police have booked him under the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (Pocso) Act.

Article Title: Sarpanch, 20 others booked for storming collectorate over BLO duty for ZP teachers
Article Text:
Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:





A village sarpanch and about 20 others have been booked for allegedly storming the district collector’s office, threatening a tehsildar and disrupting govt work while protesting the appointment of teachers from a zilla parishad school as booth-level officers for the Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar. In a separate case, a private school teacher was booked for allegedly refusing to perform SIR-related booth-level officer duties despite repeated notices.The City Chowk police registered an FIR against Mangesh Sabale, the sarpanch of Gevrai Payga village in Phulambri tehsil, and 20 unidentified people based on a complaint filed by deputy tehsildar Sanjeev Rajur. Sabale was detained by the police.According to the FIR, the group gathered at the district collectorate around 11.30am on Wednesday without permission to oppose the deployment of teachers from the village ZP school for the ongoing SIR.The complainant alleged that the protesters raised slogans, abused the district collector and Phulambri tehsildar, threatened to assault the tehsildar and disrupted official work.The FIR further stated that despite being asked to report to the police station, the protesters remained seated at the collectorate entrance, obstructing public movement. They allegedly threatened to pour petrol on themselves and commit self-immolation if detained.Based on Rajur’s complaint, the police registered a case under sections 132, 296, 126, 189 and 351(2) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and section 135 of the Maharashtra Police Act.In another case linked to the SIR exercise, the Cantonment police booked a private school teacher from the Mukundwadi area for allegedly disobeying election-related orders. A block development officer from Phulambri panchayat samiti alleged that the teacher neither responded to repeated calls from his supervisor nor accepted the appointment order when officials visited his school.The police registered a case under section 223 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and section 32 of the Representation of the People Act for allegedly failing to comply with lawful directions issued for election work.
Article Summary: Mangesh Sabale, the sarpanch of Gevrai Payga village in Phulambri tehsil, and 20 unidentified people have been booked. The group gathered at the district collectorate around 11.30am on Wednesday without permission to oppose the deployment of teachers from the village ZP school. They allegedly threatened to pour petrol on themselves and commit self-immolation if detained.

Article Title: Panvelkar firing case raised in Maharashtra Council; Sena (UBT) MLA targets govt
Article Text:
MLA raised concerns about a firing incident outside a builder's residence in Maharashtra.





KALYAN: The firing incident outside the office-cum-residence of Ambernath-based builder Vishwanath Panvelkar echoed in the Maharashtra Legislative Council on Thursday, with Shiv Sena (UBT) MLA Ambadas Danve accusing the state government of failing to ensure justice in the case.Raising the issue during the Monsoon Session, Danve alleged that a ruling party MLA from Murbad had given supari to a jailed gangster to intimidate Panvelkar. He claimed that the allegation had surfaced through a letter purportedly written by gangster Ejaz Lakdawala, but no action had been taken despite the disclosure.Danve further alleged that Panvelkar had approached the Prime Minister, the President, the Chief Minister and the police administration seeking justice, but had failed to receive any relief. He claimed that political pressure had influenced the investigation and demanded an impartial probe into the matter.The case dates back to April 21, 2025, when two unidentified motorcycle-borne assailants opened fire outside 'Sitai Sadan', Panvelkar's five-storey office-cum-residence near Hutatma Chowk in Ambernath East. Two rounds were fired, though no one was injured. The Shivajinagar police later arrested the alleged bike rider, Khalid Shahid Sayyed, and the alleged shooter, history-sheeter Jitendra Pawar alias Tiger Bhai.The investigation took a fresh turn in April 2026 after Lakdawala, lodged in jail, allegedly wrote to the Thane police commissioner claiming that associates of BJP MLA Kisan Kathore had approached him with a contract to eliminate Panvelkar over an ongoing land dispute. Panvelkar subsequently accused Kathore of orchestrating the attack.Kathore, however, in past has strongly denied the allegations, describing them as a conspiracy to tarnish his image. He has accused Panvelkar of illegally grabbing land belonging to Adivasi families and has filed a Rs 100-crore defamation suit against the
Article Summary: Shiv Sena (UBT) MLA Ambadas Danve accused the state government of failing to ensure justice in the case. Danve alleged that a ruling party MLA from Murbad had given supari to a jailed gangster to intimidate Panvelkar.

Article Title: Five to six NCP (SP) MPs anxious after split in Sena UBT, says ex-CM Prithviraj Chavan
Article Text:
Former chief mInister of Maharashtra and senior Congress functionary Prithviraj Chavan

Pune:

Supriya Sule

I NCP (SP) working president





Former chief minister and senior Congress member Prithviraj Chavan claimed there is growing unease within NCP (SP), and five to six MPs of Sharad Pawar ’s party are anxious about their future. But NCP (SP)’s working president Supriya said that all eight MPs of her party are together and ruled out any possibility of a split.Chavan made the statement while interacting with reporters, alleging that the BJP is on a spree to finish off every party in the opposition. “Modi-Shah’s govt may do the same thing with Sharad Pawar’s party as they did with Uddhav Thackeray’s party,” the Congressman said.Recently, Shiv Sena (UBT) received a major jolt when six of its MPs switched to Eknath Shinde ’s party. Referring to the second split in Uddhav’s party, Chavan said, “After six MPs switched over, there is unease among the MPs of Sharad Pawar’s party. I do not know whether they will actually switch to any Mahayuti parties, but according to my information, they are anxious.”Chavan’s comment came against the backdrop of NCP (SP) Shirur Lok Sabha MP Amol Kolhe’s recent statement during an interaction with reporters that if he gets an offer from Mahayuti, he would think about it. After his statement drew political reactions, he claimed that his statement was misconstrued.Supriya on Friday said, “I do not know what information Chavan has about our party, but I will call him to discuss and seek more details from him. All eight of our MPs remain together, travel together, and discuss all the issues among ourselves. I can assure you that nobody is anxious.”Shiv Sena (UBT) Rajya Sabha member Sanjay Raut said that Prime Minister Narendra Modi and home minister Amit Shah have been indulging in pressure politics against the opposition. “Chavan is a senior member of the Congress and he would have shared information that he has with NCP (SP) chief Sharad Pawar,” he added.I do not know what information Chavan has about our party, but I will call him to discuss and seek more details from him. All eight of our MPs remain together, travel together and discuss all the issues among ourselves. I can assure you that nobody is anxious
Article Summary: Chavan made the statement while interacting with reporters. He alleged that the BJP is on a spree to finish off every party in the opposition. But NCP (SP)’s working president Supriya said that all eight MPs of her party are together.

Article Title: Cops seize MD worth Rs 5.79 crore near Katraj; one arrested
Article Text:
Representative image

Pune:





The Pune crime branch arrested a history-sheeter and seized 3kg of mephedrone (MD) worth Rs 5.79 crore from him in the early hours of Wednesday.The accused has been identified as Prashant Sawant (38) of Lohegaon. The crime branch’s anti-narcotics cell, led by senior police inspector Trupti Sonawane, laid a trap near Katraj Chowk and apprehended Sawant. A case has been registered against him with the Bharati Vidyapeeth police.An officer said the police received a tip-off that a man carrying drugs would arrive at Katraj Chowk in the early hours of Wednesday. “We laid a trap and took him into custody. We found 3kg of MD in his bag,” the officer said.The seized drugs were valued at Rs 5.79 crore. “We are interrogating Sawant to trace the source of the contraband,” he added.
Article Summary: The accused has been identified as Prashant Sawant (38) of Lohegaon. The crime branch’s anti-narcotics cell, led by senior police inspector Trupti Sonawane, laid a trap near Katraj Chowk and apprehended Sawant.

Article Title: Six of family die in car-tanker collision near Tirupur
Article Text:
The accident happened near Uthukuli on Friday afternoon.





COIMBATORE: Six members of a family, including two children, died on the spot and a woman suffered serious injuries after their car collided head-on with a diesel tanker truck near Uthukuli in Tirupur district on Friday afternoon.The deceased have been identified as S Easwaran, 55, of Saminathapuram in Salem, his wife Thirumagal, 50, his son-in-law M Mugilan, 35, who was a college faculty member, Mugilan’s three-year-old son Srinith, his nine-month-old son Kanish, and Easwaran’s relative J Sivagami, 65. Mugilan’s wife Hema, 31, who sustained severe head injuries, was battling for life in the Tirupur Medical College Hospital, police said.According to police, Easwaran and others had gone to Palladam to attend the 30th day ritual of a deceased relative. The accident happened when they were returning to Salem via Uthukuli. Easwaran was driving the car. When the car reached near Kakkapallam close to Uthukuli, a diesel tanker truck, which was coming from the opposite direction, collided head-on with their car. The car was crushed completely, killing six occupants on the spot.After the accident, the tanker truck driver allegedly abandoned the vehicle and fled the scene.On receiving information, the Uthukuli police rushed to the spot, recovered the bodies and sent them to Tirupur Govt Medical College Hospital for postmortem. Police have registered a case and launched a search for the absconding truck driver.
Article Summary: The deceased have been identified as S Easwaran, 55, his wife Thirumagal, 50, his son-in-law M Mugilan, 35, and his two children. Mugilan’s wife Hema, 31, who sustained severe head injuries, was battling for life in the Tirupur Medical College Hospital, police said.

Article Title: Youth held for sexually harassing a woman in Coimbatore
Article Text:
Coimbatore:





A 25-year-old youth was arrested on Thursday, for sexually harassing a woman near Gandhi Nagar in Sundarapuram on Wednesday.The arrested youth has been identified as S Yuvraj, of Sundarapuram. Police said a 29-year-old woman, a resident of Sundarapuram, was walking back home after visiting her relative when the incident took place. “The youth who came on a motorcycle, touched her inappropriately and sped away when she raised an alarm,” said a police officer.However, the woman promptly noted down the motorcycle’s registration number and lodged a complaint with the Sundarapuram police. A case was registered and an investigation initiated.A police team traced the accused after analysing CCTV camera footage and arrested him. He was remanded in judicial custody. Police said he already has a robbery case pending against him.
Article Summary: A 25-year-old youth was arrested on Thursday, for sexually harassing a woman near Gandhi Nagar in Sundarapuram. The arrested youth has been identified as S Yuvraj. Police said he already has a robbery case pending against him.

Article Title: Petrol bomb hurled at restaurateur’s house near Pollachi
Article Text:
Representative image





COIMBATORE: Two miscreants hurled a petrol bomb at the residence of the owner of a chain of restaurants near Pollachi on Wednesday night.According to the police, the house belongs to Solomon of Ashok Nagar in Anaimalai. Solomon runs restaurants in Pollachi, Anaimalai and Aliyar.Two bike-borne persons wearing helmets arrived outside Solomon’s residence on Wednesday night. They filled an empty liquor bottle with fuel, attached a wick to it, lit it and hurled it at the house.Neighbours, who heard the commotion, raised an alarm. The duo sped away from the spot on the motorcycle.Based on a complaint lodged by Solomon, the Anaimalai police registered a case and conducted investigations. Police began checking footage from CCTV cameras in the locality to identify the registration number of the vehicle and track down the culprits.
Article Summary: Two bike-borne persons wearing helmets arrived outside Solomon’s residence on Wednesday night. They filled an empty liquor bottle with fuel, attached a wick to it, lit it and hurled it at the house. The duo sped away from the spot on the motorcycle.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: Five sentenced to life term for murder of Congress functionary
Article Text:
life term

Madurai:





The additional district and sessions court in Tenkasi, on Friday, convicted and sentenced five men to life imprisonment for the murder of a Congress functionary over personal enmity in 2015.The case of the prosecution is that K Samidurai of Thalaivankottai purchased a car on loan for his wife Vellaiduraichi. Since Samidurai did not repay the loan amount properly, the finance company attempted to take possession of the car. A Congress party functionary Rajagopal of Achanpudur allegedly helped to take possession of the car based on the request of the company.Samidurai and his associates kidnapped Rajagopal in a car when he was walking to his house from Achanpudur bus stop at around 9.30pm on August 12, 2015. Subsequently, they murdered Rajagopal. In a bid to destroy evidence, they chopped off his head and burnt the body. While they burnt the body near Murikulam situated on Keelanatham road, they burnt his head near the Kakkan Nagar bridge. Based on a complaint from Rajagopal’s wife, police arrested Samidurai, S Ayyappan, M Ramar, P Muthuraj and P Subbiah Pandian.The additional district and sessions judge, S Manojkumar convicted and sentenced Samidurai, Ayyappan, Ramar, Muthuraj and Subbiah to life imprisonment and imposed a fine of Rs25,000 each on them.
Article Summary: K Samidurai of Thalaivankottai purchased a car on loan for his wife Vellaiduraichi. Since he did not repay the loan amount properly, the finance company attempted to take possession of the car. A Congress party functionary Rajagopal of Achanpudur allegedly helped to take the car based on the request of the company.

Article Title: Corporation worker dies in mudslide in Madurai
Article Text:
A guest worker, Mukesh, of Bihar ho was employed on contract basis tragically died after being buried under a sudden mudslide while working on a sewage system in Zone 4 of the Madurai Corporation on Friday. (in pic) The place the worker burried in the soil at TM Nagar





Madurai: A contract guest worker from Bihar employed by the city corporation was killed after soil caved in during underground sewerage maintenance work in zone 4 on Friday morning.The deceased was Mukesh Mandal, 39, who was working as a ‘fitter’ under the corporation’s engineering division. His works included opening sewer lines and inspecting underground drainage routes, officials said.According to police, Mukesh was engaged in excavation and repair work on an underground sewer line on Friday when the soil above the trench suddenly collapsed. He was buried under the debris and died on the spot. Senior corporation officials rushed to the spot and initiated an inquiry. Authorities are examining if prescribed safety norms were followed and adequate protective measures were in place at the site. The findings of the inquiry will determine further action, officials added.Police retrieved the body and sent it to Govt Rajaji Hospital for autopsy. They registered a case and are investigating.
Article Summary: The deceased was Mukesh Mandal, 39, who was working as a ‘fitter’ under the corporation’s engineering division. Mukesh was engaged in excavation and repair work on an underground sewer line on Friday when the soil above the trench suddenly collapsed.

Article Title: Retaking of oath: Kerala HC adjourns BJP councillor’s plea to Monday
Article Text:
R Sugathan

Kochi:





Thiruvananthapuram corporation BJP councillor R Sugathan, who is currently under preventive detention under the Kerala Anti-Social Activities (Prevention) Act (Kaapa), has approached high court seeking a direction to the state govt to permit him to retake the oath of office, which is scheduled for July 14.A bench of Justice P V Kunhikrishnan on Friday adjourned the petition to July 13 for instructions from the state govt, including on whether the oath can be administered in jail. Earlier, HC had declared the oath of office taken by 20 councillors of the corporation, including Sugathan, to be void and directed them to retake the oath within four weeks. The order was passed on a petition filed by S P Deepak, another councillor, contending that the oath taken by the 20 councillors had not been administered in the prescribed form under the relevant statutes, as some of them had taken the oath in the names of various deities while others had taken it in the name of party martyrs.Meanwhile, Sugathan was arrested by police and detained under Kaapa on June 6 and has since been lodged in Viyyur Central Prison, Thrissur. The corporation has fixed July 14 for him to retake the oath of office. The judicial first-class magistrate court, Nedumangad, has also granted him interim bail to enable him to attend the oath-taking ceremony.However, Sugathan contended that despite submitting a representation to the home department additional chief secretary on July 5 seeking temporary release, no decision had been taken on his request. He apprehended that he would be prevented from attending the ceremony at the instance of certain leaders of the ruling party, who, according to him, had also precipitated the disputes in the corporation.
Article Summary: BJP councillor R Sugathan is currently under preventive detention under the Kerala Anti-Social Activities (Prevention) Act (Kaapa) Sugathan has approached high court seeking a direction to the state govt to permit him to retake the oath of office, which is scheduled for July 14.

Article Title: Kerala HC vacates order granting police protection to viral Kumbh Mela girl
Article Text:
Kochi

:





High court on Friday vacated its interim order granting police protection to the ‘viral Kumbh Mela girl’ on her petition, after the govt pleader submitted that police had been unable to locate her at the address she provided or contact her using the phone number she furnished.A bench of Justice Bechu Kurian Thomas, however, clarified that if she approached police again and sought protection in writing, they should provide her with the necessary protection. Accordingly, HC adjourned her petition seeking police protection to July 21. Earlier, on June 19, HC had directed Ernakulam Central police to provide protection to the petitioner while considering her plea. However, on Friday, the govt pleader submitted that despite repeatedly approaching the police seeking protection and furnishing her address and contact details, she could not be traced at the address provided.The petitioner alleged that after her marriage to a film actor from Madhya Pradesh, several social media handles began propagating hate campaigns against her and that she received threatening text messages from unknown persons. She further alleged that photographs of her and her husband were publicly burnt and beaten with slippers during protests, and that videos and images of these acts were circulated on various social media and online platforms.According to the petitioner, she and her husband have also been constantly followed by strangers and have been living under a constant sense of fear and insecurity.Meanwhile, a case has been registered against the husband in Madhya Pradesh on the basis of a complaint by the girl’s father, who alleged that he had abducted his daughter and that she was a minor at the time of her marriage.
Article Summary: High court vacated its interim order granting police protection to the ‘viral Kumbh Mela girl’ on her petition. The govt pleader submitted that police had been unable to locate her at the address she provided or contact her using the phone number she furnished. A case has been registered against the husband in Madhya Pradesh on the basis of a complaint.

Article Title: Kerala HC disposes of PIL against opening of Palkulamedu reserve forest for eco-tourism
Article Text:
The petitioner contended that Palkulamedu reserve forest was proposed to be opened for eco-tourism without obtaining the requisite statutory approvals from the competent authorities

Kochi:





High court has disposed of a PIL challenging the opening of Palkulamedu reserve forest for eco-tourism after recording the state govt’s submission that the site would be opened only after all mandatory statutory requirements had been complied with.A bench comprising Chief Justice Soumen Sen and Justice V M Syam Kumar was considering a PIL by M N Jayachandran of Thodupuzha. The petitioner contended that Palkulamedu reserve forest, which forms part of the Nagrampara reserve forest, was proposed to be opened for eco-tourism without obtaining the requisite statutory approvals from the competent authorities, including the eco-tourism clearance committee. He also submitted that an inauguration in connection with the proposed opening of the eco-tourism site had been held on Feb 28.However, the secretary of ministry of environment, forest and climate change filed an affidavit stating that the ministry had not yet received any proposal from Kerala govt for diversion of forest land for non-forest purposes in respect of the activities referred to in the petition. HC also noted that the project could not be made operational as the carrying capacity study and the eco-tourism plan, which are mandatory prerequisites for commencing eco-tourism activities, had not yet been completed.Further, the state govt submitted that the site would be opened only after all mandatory statutory requirements had been complied with. It also submitted that the inauguration of the project was purely ceremonial and had been held outside the forest area. Recording these submissions, HC disposed of the petition.
Article Summary: A bench comprising Chief Justice Soumen Sen and Justice V M Syam Kumar was considering a PIL by M N Jayachandran of Thodupuzha. The petitioner contended that Palkulamedu reserve forest was proposed to be opened for eco-tourism without obtaining requisite statutory approvals from the competent authorities.

Article Title: Kerala govt appoints special prosecutor to handle appeals in Renjith Sreenivasan murder case
Article Text:
Renjith Sreenivasan

Kochi:





The state govt has appointed P Vijaya Bhanu, a senior high court lawyer specialising in criminal law, as the special public prosecutor to conduct the prosecution in the appeals arising from the conviction and sentencing of the 15 accused in the 2021 Renjith Sreenivasan murder case.The appeals arise from a Mavelikkara additional sessions court judgment which convicted 15 accused in the case and sentenced them to death. The appeals are currently pending before a bench of Justices V Raja Vijayaraghavan and K V Jayakumar and are scheduled to be heard on July 21.BJP OBC Morcha state secretary Renjith Sreenivasan was brutally murdered at his home in Vellakinar, Alappuzha, on Dec 19, 2021, by a gang allegedly affiliated with the Popular Front of India (PFI) and Social Democratic Party of India (SDPI), in retaliation for the killing of SDPI state secretary K S Shan.On Jan 20, 2024, the sessions court found all 15 accused guilty of various offences and categorised the crime as falling within the ‘rarest of rare’ category. Accordingly, it sentenced all 15 accused — Naisam, Ajmal, Anoop, Mohammed Aslam, Abdul Kalam alias Salam, Abdul Kalam (of Adivaram), Safarudheen, Manshad, Jaseeb Raja, Navas, Sameer, Naseer, Sakir Hussain, Shaji alias Poovathil Shaji and Shernas Ashraf — to death, prompting them to file appeals before HC.
Article Summary: BJP OBC Morcha state secretary Renjith Sreenivasan was brutally murdered at his home in Vellakinar, Alappuzha, on Dec 19, 2021, by a gang allegedly affiliated with the Popular Front of India (PFI) and Social Democratic Party of India. The appeals are currently pending before a bench of Justices V Raja Vijayaraghavan and K V Jayakumar and are scheduled to be heard on July 21.

Article Title: Luka Doncic dragged into surprising Kylian Mbappe comparison amid France’s World Cup dominance
Article Text: Luka Doncic of the Lakers (Image via Getty)
Article Summary: Luka Doncic is one of the youngest players in NBA history. He was drafted by the L.A. Lakers in the first round of the 2013 NBA draft. He is currently playing for the Slovenian national team in the Euroleague.
